decision_target: complete keep, reshape, and remove map for every path and mixed public concept changed by PR 346
source_of_truth: git diff --name-status main...HEAD, the v3 disposition matrix, direct offline build/typecheck/test gates, authoritative SDK lint, the AGENTS graph-boundary audit, and the v2 independent rejection
read_method: Run the v3 disposition checker for the 58-path universe and the three semantic corrections; replay the direct regression and slop gates; then require a separate validator to judge the corrected architecture rows.

confidence_probability_update.reason: V3 applies the three independent validator corrections and replaces two wrong-source walls. Confidence drops because two direct test gates fail and authoritative lint plus the AGENTS audit report 74 current violations.

questions_answered: The current PR universe remains exactly 58 paths at d8dc7e845d3a7618fdf41525b67b8bac83d05d28.
The current-owned session resource declaration is kept under activation ownership and no longer carries X-PRE-RESOLVE.
Workflow state and extension declarations are separate from WorkerRegistry runtime delegation; the hidden dynamic dispatch row is reshape work owned by DKR-4.
Material state and patching are separate from cliWorker and runCli; direct node:child_process execution is reshape work owned by DKR-4 behind a required scope-substitutable process or sandbox port.
Manual pre-resolution removal remains represented in the SDK authoring, public usage, and session contract test rows.
The matrix has 90 concept rows after the two semantic splits: 48 keep, 38 reshape, and 4 remove.
The direct regression source ran 23 offline gates: 21 passed and 2 failed (test-core, test-codex).
The direct slop source scanned 76 files and reported 72 authoritative lint diagnostics plus 2 AGENTS graph-boundary violations.
Eager activation of the complete declared tree, required runtime tags, and runtime missing-tag failure remain intended and are not classified as defects.
Mechanical coverage remains separate from architectural acceptance; downstream advance stays blocked.
questions_unanswered: A separate validator must replay the v3 semantic invariants, direct walls, and all concept dispositions before DKR-0 learning can be accepted.
The package-exports test failure must be classified against its environment or baseline before any regression claim can be accepted.
The Codex ACP cancellation failures must be resolved or proven baseline before any regression claim can be accepted.
The 74 direct slop findings must be reconciled by accepted downstream work; this writer cannot waive or reclassify the wall.
DKR-1 to DKR-5 retain their existing decision targets before any product progression.
decision: candidate only; the corrected v3 matrix is replayable steering input, but direct regression and slop walls are breached and architectural judgement remains pending independent review

reasons: Direct regression gates report 2 failures against threshold 0.
Direct slop gates report 74 violations against threshold 0.
This writer cannot accept its own semantic corrections or architectural judgement.

risk_or_anti_goal_implications: The v1 marker checker is retained but no longer used as disposition-completeness evidence.
The 58-path matrix prevents README, changelog, package, provider, test-kit, build, workspace, and consumer changes from disappearing behind core-only evidence.
Concept-level rows preserve eager declared-tree activation and runtime tag binding while isolating activation, cancellation, observation, queue, and publication changes.
The cleanup-to-finishing breach remains discovery work under DKR-1; this checkpoint does not authorize a product fix.
No spawn, start, worker-pool, new ownership mode, or static missing-tag analyzer is promoted by this checkpoint.

candidate_pkrs.contribution: Apply only independently accepted DKR-1 to DKR-3 reshape and remove rows across the complete file matrix.
Apply only independently accepted DKR-4 and DKR-5 rows for queue, evidence, verification, and publication proof.
